name: concierge
displayName: CONCIERGE — Repo welcome & the idea-to-production status line
description: The ARRIVAL layer of the idea-to-production marketplace — it greets whoever opens a repo and offers the suite's status line. When a project ships a maintainer-authored .claude/welcome.md, a SessionStart hook injects it so the agent opens with a one-line greeting and a conversational decision tree of the handful of things people come there to do — operate the software, or evolve it — and routes the user to the right command, runbook, or downstream plugin. Smart-gated: it greets only on a vague/cold open and steps aside the moment the user states a concrete task, so it never nags. When a repo has no welcome yet, a second SessionStart hook makes a single, smart-gated, in-the-know offer to author one (pre-populated from the repo's CLAUDE.md/README — and, under an active idea-to-production lifecycle, the product's emergent artifacts — so it is tailored, not bland; re-offered on cold opens until accepted or declined, with a global opt-out). Under an active lifecycle the welcome is managed — stamped with its phase and auto-refreshed as the product emerges, so whoever opens the repo always meets a current front door. /concierge:define-welcome authors and refreshes that welcome by reading the repo. CONCIERGE also ships the idea-to-production STATUS LINE: /concierge:statusline turns on a rich two-line ANSI bar (context & rate-limit gauges, the product-lifecycle phase, and a ⚔ reviewer-catch tally), and on first activation it makes a single unobtrusive offer to enable it. The engine ships in the marketplace; welcome content stays repo-local, exactly like CLAUDE.md. Stands alone; lights up ideator and foundry as the 'evolve the software' destinations when they are installed.

metadata.note: skills/, commands/, agents/, and hooks/hooks.json are auto-discovered from the plugin root. /concierge:define-welcome authors a repo's .claude/welcome.md; the SessionStart hook (hooks/inject-welcome.sh) renders it when present (silent no-op when absent); a companion SessionStart hook (hooks/offer-welcome.sh) proactively offers to author one when absent and instructs a silent auto-refresh of a managed, lifecycle-stamped welcome when it falls out of step, then VERIFIES the re-stamp landed and discloses on mismatch (opt-out markers live under ~/.claude/hook-state; the hook never writes the repo). The status line ships in statusline/: /concierge:statusline runs statusline/install.sh (copies the version-stamped i2p-statusline.sh → ~/.claude, points settings.json at it); a PostToolUse hook (statusline/count-adversarial-catches.sh) feeds the ⚔ caught tally from the shared statusline/adversarial-artifacts.lst glob set; SessionStart hooks offer the status line once (hooks/offer-statusline.sh) and detect installed-vs-shipped renderer drift (hooks/check-statusline-drift.sh, offers /concierge:statusline to refresh). The TOKEN-AWARE SCHEDULER ships in scheduler/ (skills/token-scheduler + /concierge:schedule): a fair, defensive guardian for a solo builder's usage meter — it estimates a wide fan-out's cost (scheduler-estimate.sh + the calibrate.sh estimate→actual EWMA over the shared ~/.claude/state/i2p-cost/calibration.json), gates every throttled wave against the LIVE rolling rate-limit window (ceiling-check.sh, fail-closed), halts before a paid lockout, and resumes cheaply from a per-job ledger (.i2p/jobs/<id>.json, job-ledger.sh) — with supervised off-peak (22:00–08:00) runs that reserve a morning allowance (offpeak-window.sh + offpeak-budget.sh). The live signal reaches non-hook code via a freshness-stamped snapshot (scheduler/ratelimit-snapshot.sh, registered on SessionStart/PostToolUse/Stop; no-op when the payload lacks rate_limits). A SessionStart hook offers it once (hooks/offer-scheduler.sh). scheduler/preflight-fanout.sh (a PreToolUse Agent|Task backstop) and scheduler/verify-payload.sh (the [VERIFY] probe) ship UNREGISTERED — opt-in after confirming which events carry .rate_limits. The deterministic core is 100%-covered by a zero-dependency pure-bash suite (scheduler/test/run.sh; needs only bash+jq+awk). Canonical reference: knowledge/token-aware-scheduling.md. /concierge:check probes tool deps (skills/check/requirements.tsv) and /concierge:inspect runs the inspector agent. Carries the byte-identical SOUL.md + inject-soul.sh + check.sh + inspection-core.md canonical copies (CI verify-prereqs.sh checks A/E/F).
